commentary: this is 1 of the important sutas to show that the buddha and his disciples did eat midna and because the buddha and his disciples ate midna as compared to some of the external ascetics who were vegetarians they used this to disparage the buddha and you can see from here they were very angry that 1 of the chief disciples had crossed over to the buddha's camp so when they found that siha had ordered meat for to to feed the buddha they went around broadcasting that the buddha was going to eat meat that was purposely killed for him and that is why you find later when we come to the majima nikayasutas there was a physician a king's physician by the name of jivaca in 1 of the majima nikaya sutas he came to ask the buddha he also heard things like this like that the buddha ate meat that was purposely killed for him so he asked the buddha whether it's true and the buddha said no it's not true the buddha said that the buddha and his monks only ate meat now with 3 conditions the first 1 was they did not kill did not ask somebody to kill in other words the meat was not purposely killed for the buddha and his disciples you know because if they buy it from the market it's all right and the second condition they don't see the killing the third condition they don't hear the animal cry as it was being killed [music] you notice here the general said not for the sake of sustaining life would we intentionally deprive any being of life so in other words the meat that the cooker was bought from the marketplace that was already slaughtered the reason for this is that there is it is almost impossible to cause in not to cause indirectly indirect killing of animals and just by existing we unintentionally cause the killing of other living beings lives for example even if a person is a vegetarian still for the farmer to grow the vegetables and the fruits a lot of waters have to be cleared in the process many animals are killed and when the farmer protects his crops his praise insecticides and he tills the ground and all that a lot of worms are killed a lot of caterpillars a lot of snails have to be purposely killed insects etc to protect the vegetables and all that and then like we want to live in houses just by the fact that we want to live in houses many areas and large areas of land have to be cleared to build housing estates that way we indirectly cause the killing of animals and then because we want to use household items also a lot of land has to be cleared in an animal skill and because we want to use electricity the government has to make dams to dam up the rivers and when the rivers are dammed up and the water level rises a lot of animals are killed in the process also we know for example in malaysia every day the government employs people to go and shoot stray drugs wine to protect us so if you allow stray dogs to multiply and then the result is that there'll be a lot of disease and it would affect humans so in the same way if animals were not slaughtered then you'll allow them to be freed then they multiply so fast and that somehow they have to be killed we know like in australia every every day thousands and thousands of kangaroos and rabbits are killed because they eat the crops so if animals are allowed to multiply they will eat all the crops and the farmers will also eat them if everybody became vegetarians the whole world became vegetarians too animals would still be killed the fact that we drive cars is contributing to the killing of animals countless animals every day the fact that you you you drive a car you're encouraging the car industry to make more cars and when more cars are made then more animals are killed so in indirectly you're also responsible for it and not only animals human beings are also killed by cars in fact 2 000 human beings are killed by cars every day on the road in this world so the fact that we drive cars or we sit in cars we are indirectly causing the killing of human beings and animals on the road because we encourage the production of more cars every day yeah so because of that now you can see here that killing is something that indirect killing is something that cannot be avoided just because we want to survive but what is important is that the direct karma of killing should not be on our hands and the buddha said now for that there must be these 3 conditions if we want to eat meat we don't kill we don't ask somebody to kill we don't see the killing and we don't hear the animal cry as he was being killed and then our hands are in a wave of this killing huh
sutta: 8.2.12 once the exalted 1 was dwelling in the gable hall in mahavana near vaisali now at that time a great many elichavi nobles notables were seated assembled in the mode hall and in many a figure they were speaking in praise of the buddha in praise of dhamma in praise of the sangha and siha the general the disciple of the nigantas were seated in that assembly and he thought surely the exalted 1 must be arahan samasam buddha thus indeed these many notable lichavis assembled and seated in their mood hall in many a figure praise the buddha praise dhamma praise the sangha now suppose i were to go and see him the exalted 1 arahan sama sambuddha i'll just stop here for a moment just to commend this hall can't find the word mode in the dictionary probably something like a town hall uh and these uh licheby's uh it's a a race and they were praising the triple gem now this siha the general that means he's a very important man the general of the army he was a disciple of the nigantas are also called the genes and the leader is known as nattaputta that means son of natta and was probably his mother he's also sometimes known as mahabhira his real name was probably mahavira but sometimes he's also called nataputta and to continue the suta then siha approached the niganta nataputta and said to him sir i wish to go and see the recluse gotama and nataputa said see her how can you who believe in action go and see the recruits gautama who affirms the theory of inaction the recluse gautama professes the theory of inaction he teaches the doctrine of inaction and in that he trains his disciples then whatever inclination siha had to go and see the exalted 1 subsided on the second occasion the lichavis were speaking in praise of the buddha dharma and the sangha and again siha told nathaputa of his desire to go and see the exalted 1 and was again dissuaded on the third occasion the lichevies were praising the buddha dharma and sangha in like manner and thought siha and undoubtedly that exalted 1 is arahan speak in praise of him of the dhamma and the sangha what can the nigantas do to me whether they are consulted or not what if i go without the nigantas leave and see that exalted 1 so at noon seeha set out from vezali with some 500 chariots to see the exalted 1 going by carriage as far as the ground permitted and then descending there from entered the park on foot approaching the exalted 1 he saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 lord i have heard this i have heard this the recluse gautama affirms the theory of inaction he teaches the doctrine of inaction and in that he trains his disciples lord those who speak thus i presume they report what the exalted 1 has said and do not misrepresent the exalted 1 by lying i presume they set forth a doctrine which conforms to dhamma and no 1 who is a follower of his dhamma would incur blame for saying this lord i have no wish to accuse the exalted 1 and the buddha said there is 1 way siha in which 1 might say if he would speak rightly of me the recluse gotama affirms the theory of inaction he teaches the doctrine of inaction and in that he trains his disciples there is 1 way siha in which 1 might say if he would speak rightly of me the recluse gautama affirms the theory of action he teaches the doctrine of action and in that he trains his disciples in 1 way 1 mind 1 might rightly say the recluse gautama is an annihilationist he teaches the doctrine of annihilation and in that he trains his disciples or he feels abhorrence he teaches the doctrine of abhorrence he is an abolitionist he teaches the doctrine of abolition he is given to mortification he teaches the doctrine of mortification he is against rebirth he teaches the doctrine of preventing rebirth he has found consolation he teaches the doctrine of consolation and in that he trains his disciples and what siha is the way in which speaking rightly of me 1 would say the recluse gotama affirms the theory of inaction i declare in action as to misconduct in deed word and thought i proclaim in action as to all evil and sinful conditions this is the way siha in which speaking rightly of me 1 would say the recluse gautama affirms the theory of inaction he teaches the doctrine of inaction and in that way he trains his disciples in what way would 1 say the recruits gautama affirms the theory of action i declare action as to good condom indeed word and thought i proclaim action as to all righteous conditions this is the way in what way would 1 say the recluse gautama is an annihilationist i declare the annihilation of lust hatred and delusion i proclaim the annihilation of all evil and sinful conditions this is the way in what way would 1 say the recluse gautama feels abhorrence i pour misconduct indeed word and thought i abhor entertaining all evil and sinful conditions this is the way in what we would once say the recruits go to mice and abolitionists i preach the doctrine of abolishing lust hatred and delusion i teach the dhamma of abolishing all evil and sinful conditions this is the way in what way would 1 say the recluse gautama is given to mortification i declare all evil and sinful conditions should be mortified yes this conduct indeed word and thought moreover whosoever has abandoned rooted out made like palm stumps caused not to be so conditioned that they arise not again evil and sinful conditions he i say is given to mortification the tatar gather has so done this is the way in what we would once say the recluse gotama is against rebirth whosoever has abandoned cut off at the root descend into the womb renewed becoming a rebirth he i say is against rebirth the tatragata has so done this is the way and what seeha is the rightly of me 1 would say the recluse gutama has found consolation he teaches dhamma for consolation and with that he trains his disciples i see her have found consolation by the supreme consolation i teach dhamma for consolation and in that i train my disciples this is the way siha in which speaking rightly of me 1 would say the recluse gautama has found consolation he teaches dhamma for consolation and in that he trains his disciples and when he had finished speaking siha the general said wonderful wonderful lord etc lord let the exalted 1 accept me as a lay disciple as 1 who has found refuge from this day to life's end and the buddha said make a thorough examination of the matter see her investigation is profitable to well-known men like yourself and he said lord i am even better pleased better gratified by this remark of the exalted 1 had i been won over as a disciple by some other sect they would have paraded through the whole of vaisali shouting see how the general has joined our discipleship but the exalted 1 merely advises me thus examine the matter siha for investigation is profitable to well-known men like yourself for the second time lord i go to the exalted 1 for refuge to the dhamma and to the sangha of monks let the exalted me let the exalted 1 accept me as a lay disciple as 1 who has found a refuge from this day to life's end and the buddha said your family seeha for many a day has been a wellspring to the negantas therefore deem it right to give arms to those who approach you and see her said lord i am still better pleased better gratified by this last remark lord i have heard it said the recluse gotama asserts thus not unto others but unto me alone should arms be given not unto the disciples of others but unto mine alone should arms be given for alms given unto me alone are very fruitful and not sure are those given unto others arms given unto my disciples alone are very fruitful and not so are those given unto the disciples of others but now the exalted 1 incites me to give alms among the nikanthas we shall know what to do when the time arrives for a third time lord i go to the exalted 1 for refuge to the dhamma and to the sangha of monks let the exalted 1 accept me as a lay disciple from this day to life's end i just stopped here for a moment you is you notice here um that shiha being a general uh was a vip a very important person in vegas ali yet the buddha asked him to think over carefully when he wanted to become a disciple then the buddha advised him to continue giving arms to the nigantas so we can see the compassion and the wisdom of the buddha anyone who is practicing correctly must have the basic humane qualities of good will sympathy or compassion humility etc but if his anger increases the ego increases and he does not possess basic humane qualities he must be on the wrong path and then the suta continues then the exalted 1 preached a graduated discourse to see her the general that is to say on arms giving the precepts and so and and on heaven he set forth the peril of the folly and the depravity of lusts and the blessedness of renunciation and when the exalted 1 knew that the heart of siha the general was clear malleable free from hindrance uplifted and lucid then he revealed the teaching of dhamma which buddhas alone have won that is to say dukkha it's arising its ending and the way to the cessation of dukkha just as a clean cloth free of all stain will take die perfectly even so in siha the general seated there that arose the spotless stainless vision of dhamma that whatsoever be conditioned by coming to be all that is subject to ending i'll stop here for a moment here you see it's mentioned in this that the buddha preached a graduated discourse to the siha [music] and these 9 steps first 1 is dharna then sila then heaven then the peril folly and depravity of sensual desire then renunciation these are the 5 steps after which are the 4 noble truths and this particular gradual discourse is not found as such per se in the sutures and it's not it's not given but if you look through the sutas in various parts of the suit you can find a description of some of these things and you also notice that when the buddha had given this gradual discourse to see her the general then it is said that the spotless stainless vision of dhamma arose that means uh the see how the general attained stream entry attained path the the the first path became an area and you can see here in the sutras and in the vinaya that almost all of the people who attain the first path they do so by hearing the dhamma understanding and then the buddha said they attain stream entry and the sutta continues then see her perceiving dhamma in possession of dhamma discovering dhamma penetrating dhamma without overcome and uncertainty surpassed possessed without another's aid of perfect confidence in the word of the teacher said to the exalted 1 lord let the exalted 1 with the sangha of monks accept a meal from me tomorrow and the exalted 1 accepted by silence there was the normal ways they stopped here to come in that was the normal way and during the buddha's time if the monks and the buddha and these monks were invited to a meal and they kept quiet and then they accepted it i forgot to mention also uh that in in in this suta in the previous 1 that when they take refuge in the buddha dharma and sangha is mentioned that they take refuge with the bhikkhu sangha usually when they take refuge as mentioned here they take refuge in the bhikkhu sangha [music] then the suta continues then see her seeing that the exalted 1 had accepted cut up from his seat saluted the exalted 1 and departed by the right and siha called the man and said go a good man and find some fresh meat and when that night was over having had plenty of hard and soft food prepared in his house he sent word to the exalted 1 that it was time to come lord the time has come the meal is ready in siha's house so the exalted 1 robbing himself early in the morning taking bowl and cloak went to see her's house with the sangha of monks and sat down on the appointed sea then many nigantas went about their sally through the highways and byways from crossroads to crossroads waving their arms and crying today a huge beast has been slain by siha the general and a meal has been prepared for the recluse guatemala and the recruits gautama is going to eat the meat knowing that it was meant for him that the deed was done on his account then a man went and whispered in siha's ear saying i say sir are you aware that a great many nigantas go about the streets and cross roads of the asali waving their arms crying in such a manner and she said enough sir for a long time these worthies have long to disperse the buddha have long to disperse the dhamma have long to disperse the sangha but they do no harm to the exalted 1 by their wicked vain lying untruthful slanders not for the sake of sustaining life would we intentionally deprive any being of life then see her the general with his own hands served and satisfied the sangha of monks with the buddha at their head with plenty of food both hard and soft and when the exalted 1 had finished eating and had withdrawn his hand from the bowl siha sat down at 1 side associated the exalted 1 instructed siha with dhamma discourse stimulated him roused him and gladdened him then the exalted 1 arose from his seat and departed that's the end of the sutta
